questions.question: Which haematological neoplasms shows Auer rods on morphology ?
Which of the following is a major clue used in plasma cell myeloma diagnosis ?
Which of the following chromosomal translocations is present in chronic myeloid leukaemia ?
Which of the following haematological malignancies is characterized by the presence of Philadelphia chromosome ?
Which of the following is a specific marker of B cells ?
Which of the following is a major criterion in essential thrombocythemia ?
questions.correct_answer: Increase platelets count more than 450 x10⁹ / L
wrong_answers: Occurs only in younger age groups

questions.question: A 6-year-old boy presets with a 4-week history of fatigue and fever and 1-week history of bruising and pallor. Laboratory evaluation shows pancytopenia. Bone marrow aspiration shows myeloblasts with granules and occasional Auer rod. What is the most likely diagnosis ?
Which cell is specific to primary myelofibrosis ?
A patient came with absolute lymphocytosis and upon microscopic examination , smudge cells were detected. Which of the following is the most common diagnosis ?
Which of the following haematological neoplasms correlates with this blood/BM findings ?
Which of the following correlates with Philadelphia chromosome ?
questions.correct_answer: Translocation between long arm of chromosome 9 and long arm of chromosome 22
wrong_answers: Translocation between short arm of chromosome 9 and short arm of chromosome 22
Translocation between long arm of chromosome 9 and short arm of chromosome 22.
Translocation between short arm of chromosome 9 and long arm of chromosome 22

questions.question: A 70 year old man came with plethora, fatigue and headache. HCT and Hb values were elevated. What is the most probable diagnosis ?

Which diagnostic method is used to confirm B cell monoclonality of CLL ?
What is the difference between CLL and SLL ?
Detection of smudge cells on a blood smear may indicate which of the following conditions ?
A M-protein concentration of 25g/L with no organ involvement correlates with which of the following ?
An absolute number of 6x10^9/L of circulating plasma cells and a percentage of 25% is seen in which of the following ?
Which of the following haematological neoplasms correlates with this blood/BM findings ?

What is the predominant cell population in plasma cell dyscriasis ?
Active tyrosine kinase is responsible for cytoplasmic and nuclear signals. This explains the increased cell proliferation in which of the following ?
Trilineage proliferation with prominent erythroid and megakaryocytic series is seen in which of the following ?
Which of the following is true about essential thrombocythemia ?
questions.correct_answer: Splenomegaly is seen in 50% of patients
wrong_answers: It's only manifested by episodes of thrombosis
Patients are mainly symptomatic with predominance among adults
The main diagnostic method is conventional cytogenetics

questions.question: Which of the following is true regarding PMF ?
questions.correct_answer: Bone marrow fibrosis is mainly reactive
wrong_answers: Trilineage proliferation with predominance of erythroid lines only
Hematopoesis is mainly carried out by bone marrow
